perturbation.original_text: Exhibit 10.4 FORM OF TRANSFER AND SERVICING AGREEMENT among VERIZON OWNER TRUST 2020-A, as Issuer, VERIZON ABS LLC, as Depositor and CELLCO PARTNERSHIP d/b/a VERIZON WIRELESS, as Servicer, Marketing Agent and Custodian Dated as of January 29, 2020 Source: VERIZON ABS LLC, 8-K, 1/23/2020
perturbation.changed_text: Exhibit 10.4 FORM OF TRANSFER AND SERVICING AGREEMENT among VERIZON OWNER TRUST 2020-A, as Issuer, VERIZON ABS LLC, as Depositor and CELLCO PARTNERSHIP d/b/a VERIZON WIRELESS, as Account Manager, Marketing Agent and Custodian Dated as of January 29, 2020 Source: VERIZON ABS LLC, 8-K, 1/23/2020
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Servicer' to 'Account Manager' introduces confusion. While Cellco is initially defined as the 'Servicer', subsequent sections outline responsibilities typically associated with a servicer. Using 'Account Manager' creates uncertainty about whether Cellco's servicing duties are fully replaced or merely renamed, potentially impacting enforcement of the Servicer's obligations. The word 'servicer' is mentioned and defined throughout the document and by changing it to 'Account Manager' creates in-text contradictions
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 3.2 Servicing of Receivables. (a) General Servicing Obligations. The Servicer will manage, service, administer and collect on the Receivables with reasonable care using that degree of skill and attention that the Servicer exercises for all comparable device payment plan agreement receivables that it services for itself or others according to the Servicing Procedures. Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Servicer's obligations will include: (i) collecting and applying all payments made on, or credits applied to, the Receivables and any other amounts received related to the Depositor Transferred Property; (ii) investigating delinquencies; (iii) sending invoices and notices and responding to inquiries of Obligors; (iv) processing requests for extensions, modifications and adjustments; (v) administering payoffs, prepayments, defaults and delinquencies; (vi) maintaining accurate and complete accounts and receivables systems for servicing the Receivables; (vii) providing to the Custodian copies, or access to, any documents that modify or supplement information in the Receivable Files; and (viii) preparing and providing Monthly Investor Reports and any other periodic reports required to be prepared by the Servicer under this Agreement or any other Transaction Document.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines Cellco Partnership as the 'Servicer.' The changed text replaces 'Servicer' with 'Account Manager.' The contradicted text, Section 3.2, explicitly outlines the 'Servicer's' obligations. By changing the defined term, it creates an in-text contradiction. The contract uses 'Servicer' in Section 3.2, creating uncertainty about whether Cellco, now called 'Account Manager,' is still responsible for the obligations detailed in Section 3.2. This misalignment in terminology leads to conflicting obligations, as it's unclear if the obligations are now assigned to the Account Manager. This fits the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' because the contract uses 'Servicer' and 'Account Manager' potentially interchangeably without defining them as the same entity, leading to conflicting obligations, specifically in section 3.2. This creates a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Section 3.2 Servicing of Receivables. (a) General Servicing Obligations. The Servicer will manage, service, administer and collect on the Receivables with reasonable care using that degree of skill and attention that the Servicer exercises for all comparable device payment plan agreement receivables that it services for itself or others according to the Servicing Procedures.
perturbation.changed_text: Section 3.2 Servicing of Receivables. (a) General Collection Obligations. The Collector will manage, service, administer and collect on the Receivables with reasonable care using that degree of skill and attention that the Collector exercises for all comparable device payment plan agreement receivables that it services for itself or others according to the Collection Procedures.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Servicing' to 'Collection' and 'Servicer' to 'Collector' in the header of the clause, it creates a contradiction because throughout the text, the use of term `Servicer` is more often, that `Collector` and they never defined, they are the same entity. They are two different obligations, collection and servicing.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 3.2 Servicing of Receivables. (a) General Collection Obligations. The Collector will manage, service, administer and collect on the Receivables with reasonable care using that degree of skill and attention that the Collector exercises for all comparable device payment plan agreement receivables that it services for itself or others according to the Collection Procedures. Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Servicer's obligations will include: (i) collecting and applying all payments made on, or credits applied to, the Receivables and any other amounts received related to the Depositor Transferred Property; (ii) investigating delinquencies; (iii) sending invoices and notices and responding to inquiries of Obligors; (iv) processing requests for extensions, modifications and adjustments; (v) administering payoffs, prepayments, defaults and delinquencies; (vi) maintaining accurate and complete accounts and receivables systems for servicing the Receivables; (vii) providing to the Custodian copies, or access to, any documents that modify or supplement information in the Receivable Files; and (viii) preparing and providing Monthly Investor Reports and any other periodic reports required to be prepared by the Servicer under this Agreement or any other Transaction Document.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Servicer' to 'Collector' in the header and body of Section 3.2 creates a contradiction because the contradicted text explicitly refers to the 'Servicer' and their obligations, while the modified text refers to the 'Collector'. Although the explanation mentions the entities may be the same, the contradiction is that the text explicitly defines 'Servicer' obligations in the contradicted text. Changing 'Servicer' to 'Collector' and 'Servicing' to 'Collection' introduces conflicting terminology in the contradicted text because both terms are being used to describe the same set of actions. This discrepancy creates an ambiguity regarding who is responsible for what, creating a strong contradiction, falling under the Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Section 3.5 Servicer Reports and Compliance Statements. (a) Monthly Investor Report. (i) On or about the 15th day of each month, and in no case later than at least two (2) Business Days before each Payment Date, the Servicer will deliver to the Depositor, the Indenture Trustee, the Owner Trustee, the Note Paying Agent, the Cap Counterparty, the Rating Agencies and the Administrator a servicing report (the "Monthly Investor Report") for that Payment Date and the related Collection Period. The Monthly Investor Report will include (i) an Acquisition Date Supplement if the Collection Period includes an Acquisition Date and (ii) a statement as to whether or not a Delinquency Trigger has occurred in respect of the related Collection Period, together with reasonably detailed calculations thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: Section 3.5 Servicer Reports and Compliance Statements. (a) Monthly Investor Report. (i) On or about the 15th day of each month, and in no case later than at least two (2) Business Days before each Payment Date, the Monitor will deliver to the Depositor, the Indenture Trustee, the Owner Trustee, the Note Paying Agent, the Cap Counterparty, the Rating Agencies and the Administrator a servicing report (the "Monthly Investor Report") for that Payment Date and the related Collection Period. The Monthly Investor Report will include (i) an Acquisition Date Supplement if the Collection Period includes an Acquisition Date and (ii) a statement as to whether or not a Delinquency Trigger has occurred in respect of the related Collection Period, together with reasonably detailed calculations thereof.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Servicer' to 'Monitor' creates ambiguity. While the 'Servicer' is clearly defined, introducing 'Monitor' without defining it suggests a separate entity overseeing the Servicer's reports. This change implies a lack of consistency, potentially creating confusion about which entity is responsible for the reports. This change creates a in-text contradiction, cause 'servicer' is mentioned every where else
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 3.5 Servicer Reports and Compliance Statements. (a) Monthly Investor Report. (i) On or about the 15th day of each month, and in no case later than at least two (2) Business Days before each Payment Date, the Monitor will deliver to the Depositor, the Indenture Trustee, the Owner Trustee, the Note Paying Agent, the Cap Counterparty, the Rating Agencies and the Administrator a servicing report (the "Monthly Investor Report") for that Payment Date and the related Collection Period. The Monthly Investor Report will include (i) an Acquisition Date Supplement if the Collection Period includes an Acquisition Date and (ii) a statement as to whether or not a Delinquency Trigger has occurred in respect of the related Collection Period, together with reasonably detailed calculations thereof. A Responsible Person of the Servicer will certify that the information in the Monthly Investor Report is accurate in all material respects. The Monthly Investor Report will also be posted on the Indenture Trustee's password protected website located at https://pivot.usbank.com.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Servicer' to 'Monitor' in Section 3.5(a)(i) creates a direct contradiction with the contradicted text in Section 3.5(a)(i) which states, 'A Responsible Person of the Servicer will certify that the information in the Monthly Investor Report is accurate in all material respects.' The modified text implies that the 'Monitor' delivers the report, while the contradicted text states that the 'Servicer' certifies the report's accuracy, creating confusion and inconsistency about which entity is ultimately responsible for the accuracy of the Monthly Investor Report, thus creating an in-text contradiction and falling under the Misaligned Terminology definition. The original text explicitly identified the Servicer as the responsible party for delivering and certifying the report. The change introduces a second, undefined party called the Monitor.
